# /pitch

Next.js route segment implementing error boundaries, loading states, and page mounting for the pitch viewer UI.

## Contents

### Route Handlers
- [**page.tsx**](./page.tsx) — mounts `PitchViewer` component as default export for `/pitch` route
- [**loading.tsx**](./loading.tsx) — renders `PitchLoading` skeleton UI with header bar (h-14, bg-card, border-b) and 3 placeholder cards during Suspense fallback
- [**error.tsx**](./error.tsx) — exports `PitchError({ reset })` boundary rendering centered Card with "Something went wrong" message, processing guidance text, and "Try again" retry button

## Architecture

Route segment in Next.js file-based routing tree. `page.tsx` imports `PitchViewer` from `@/components/viewer/pitch-viewer`, delegating all viewer logic to the component layer. `loading.tsx` and `error.tsx` provide declarative UI states for async transitions and runtime failures per Next.js App Router conventions.

## UI Structure

Loading state: fullscreen min-h-screen bg-muted flex layout with max-w-5xl centered container (py-16 px-4), maps 3 skeleton cards with p-8 spacing, each containing full-width h-4, 4/5-width h-4, and 3/5-width h-3 `Skeleton` components from `@/components/ui/skeleton`.

Error state: fullscreen min-h-screen bg-muted flex layout with centered Card, destructive text styling for "Something went wrong", guidance text "Unable to load pitch content. Check that your documents have finished processing, then refresh the page.", and reset button labeled "Try again".

## Integration Points

- **Component Import:** `PitchViewer` from `@/components/viewer/pitch-viewer`
- **UI Components:** `Skeleton` from `@/components/ui/skeleton`, `Card` and `Button` (implied by error.tsx structure)
- **Next.js Conventions:** file-based routing, error boundaries, Suspense loading states
